raw.objective: “we explored the applicability of counter-propagation artificial neural networks (CPANNs) combined with genetic algorithm (GA) for prediction of TPM serum levels based on selected factors important for its prediction”
raw.covariates: Tested: smoking status, co-therapy with lamotrigine, levetiracetam, valproic acid,CBZ, benzodiazepines and risperidone; daily TPM dose, renal function (eGFR), liver enzymes (AST, ALP and cholinesterase), bilirubin, albumin, total protein concentration, daily CBZ dose Included: TPM dose, renal function (eGFR) and carbamazepine
raw.observations: TPM dose, renal function (eGFR) and carbamazepine dose significantly influenced TPM levels. Results indicate the feasibility of CPANNs combined with GA to predict TPM concentrations and to adjust relative importance of identified variability factors in population of adult epileptic patients. Results are in line with previous population PK analyses. CPANNs models can be useful to detect factors influencing drug levels even in the absence of prior knowledge on drug’s PK. The approach may be useful for initial covariate screening. Unfortunately neither structure nor parameter values were reported for the model (other than the number of neurons used).
raw.equations: No structure reported, except for the use of 20 neurons
raw.validation: No / Yes (training and test datasets used for model building and validation)

extraction_target.title: Application of Counter-propagation Artificial Neural Networks in Prediction of Topiramate Concentration in Patients with Epilepsy.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “we explored the applicability of counter-propagation artificial neural networks (CPANNs) combined with genetic algorithm (GA) for prediction of TPM serum levels based on selected factors important for its prediction”

covariates.raw: Tested: smoking status, co-therapy with lamotrigine, levetiracetam, valproic acid,CBZ, benzodiazepines and risperidone; daily TPM dose, renal function (eGFR), liver enzymes (AST, ALP and cholinesterase), bilirubin, albumin, total protein concentration, daily CBZ dose Included: TPM dose, renal function (eGFR) and carbamazepine

validation.raw: No / Yes (training and test datasets used for model building and validation)

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
